core_mechanism: Reconstruction of a pointwise (instantaneous) rate field from its interval-averaged form via a differential identity relating an interval average to the instantaneous value and the derivative of that average, so that an iterative improvement operator defined on the instantaneous field can be applied to a model that only outputs the interval-averaged field while sampling still uses the average
brief_reason: The method's whole bridge is the 'MeanFlow identity' relating average and instantaneous velocities, from which an induced instantaneous-velocity predictor is constructed so an objective defined on instantaneous rates becomes well-defined for a model that predicts interval-averaged rates, with a proven monotone improvement guarantee.
